title: Active Nav Tab Not Syncing with URL
author: Amin

blocks.content:
#### What Was Happening

The navbar used a local React state (`activeTab`) to track which tab was "active":

js

```plain
const [activeTab, setActiveTab] = useState("Home");
```

It only updated when the user **clicked directly on a nav link**:

js

```plain
onClick={() => setActiveTab(item.name)}
```

So if navigation happened from **anywhere else** — a button, a redirect, a programmatic `router.push()`, or a back/forward browser action — the URL would change, but `activeTab` stayed the same. The highlighted tab was out of sync with the actual page.

***

#### Root Cause

The active tab was driven by **component state**, not by the **actual URL**. These two sources of truth can easily diverge in Next.js apps where navigation can be triggered from many places.

***

#### The Fix

Replace the manual state with `usePathname()` from `next/navigation`, which always reflects the current URL:

js

```plain
const pathname = usePathname();
```

Then derive `isActive` directly from it:

js

```plain
const isActive = pathname === item.url;
```

Now there's **a single source of truth** — the URL itself. No matter how the user navigates (clicking a nav link, pressing a button, using the browser back/forward, or a programmatic redirect), the navbar always reads the current path and highlights the correct tab automatically.

***

#### Key Takeaway

> Never use local state to track something that is already represented in the URL. In Next.js, `usePathname()` gives you that value reactively — use it instead of maintaining a parallel state that can go out of sync.
